close_reason: Positive observation: URL-component validation is correct
description:
src/lib.rs:190-206 validate_gcp_resource_name_component rejects characters not in [A-Za-z0-9_-] for project, secret, version. Sibling backends (azure-kv, doppler, vault) only check emptiness/extra-slash; they accept e.g. '#' and '%' in the path (post percent-decode in SecretUri::parse).

Why this matters: SecretUri::parse percent-decodes the path. So 'secretx:gcp-sm:my-project%23attack/secret' decodes to project='my-project#attack' which, if interpolated raw into the REST URL, would corrupt the URL fragment boundary. Without the validator the resulting URL would be 'projects/my-project#attack/secrets/.../versions/latest:access' — the '#' truncates the path, sending the request to a different endpoint entirely.

Tests at src/lib.rs:443-471 specifically anchor this behavior with %23/%25 cases. Those tests should stay.

A 'simplification' that removed the validator would re-open this URL-injection surface. Defend.

Cross-reference: there is a separate finding (priority-2) that the validator could be tightened per-component; but that strengthens, not removes, this guard.
title: Defend: char-class URL-component validation; only backend doing it
